conflicts.conflictClass: generated adapter versus cold-start discovery conflict

GSAP skill forbids login/static-background motion and limits login to Level 1 motion by default, but LoginShell.vue and AuthShaderBackdrop.vue use GSAP timelines for login entrance and background bubble drift.
conflicts.evidence: ccd-gsap-motion/SKILL.md line 39; ccd-motion-system/SKILL.md line 82; LoginShell.vue lines 14-92; AuthShaderBackdrop.vue lines 15-52.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Authoritative protocol includes ccd-page-archetypes while generic UI route omits it
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: adapter-manifest.json codex.skillMapping design chain includes ccd-page-archetypes; skill-routing.json route ccd-ui-design skills list does not include it.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Pages routed through ccd-ui-design may not load ccd-page-archetypes skill unless task-orchestrator or adapter-manifest heuristics trigger it separately.
knownFindings.risk: Inconsistent design skill loading; page archetype guidance may be skipped for generic UI tasks.
knownFindings.directEvidence: adapter-manifest.json lines 67-68 list ccd-page-archetypes; skill-routing.json route ccd-ui-design skills array lines 83-90 omits it.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Generic UI route loads GSAP and animate-lite too broadly
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: skill-routing.json ccd-ui-design route skills include both ccd-gsap-motion and ccd-animate-lite.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Every UI/UX task triggers both motion skills, even when only simple transitions are needed.
knownFindings.risk: Token bloat and potential override of reduced-motion defaults; GSAP guidance loaded for tasks that do not need it.
knownFindings.directEvidence: skill-routing.json route ccd-ui-design skills array lines 83-90.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Several routing globs still use old single-root paths such as src/views/**, src/router/**, src/hooks/**, vite.config.ts, and src-tauri/**
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: rule-index.json and rule frontmatter contain 42 old single-root globs (src/**, src/views/**, src/router/modules/**, src/hooks/**, vite.config.ts, src-tauri/**, etc.) with zero or partial matches in the monorepo layout.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Rules intended for web-demo source files do not automatically match apps/web-demo/src/** paths.
knownFindings.risk: AI clients may not load intended rules for monorepo app packages.
knownFindings.directEvidence: glob-evidence.json summary and glob entries for src/views/**, src/router/modules/**, src/hooks/**, etc.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Existing UI Skill precedence allows .ai/rules/** and existing implementation patterns to outrank the project design Skill
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: Current repository precedence: .ai/protocol/AGENTS.core.md establishes load order, but .ai/rules/** and owner decisions are observed as the highest authority for enforced constraints. Existing design Skills (ccd-product-language, ccd-material-system, ccd-motion-system, ccd-page-archetypes, ccd-ui-review-gate, ccd-gsap-motion, ccd-animate-lite) are explicitly subordinate to .ai/rules/** and UIDesignState in protocol text. No project-ui Skill exists, so no observed precedence for a nonexistent Skill. Implementation patterns may contradict Skill guidance (e.g., login GSAP), which demonstrates that implementation evidence can override Skill authority in practice.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Project UI profile cannot override platform invariants or existing rules.
knownFindings.risk: Future project-ui skill must be designed to be subordinate to rules, not authoritative over them.
knownFindings.evidence: AGENTS.core.md lines 154-165; ccd-ui-review-gate/SKILL.md; ccd-product-language/SKILL.md constraint clauses.

knownFindings.originalClaim: UIDesignState describes a page contract but has no verified runtime consumer
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: design-state.ts header comments explicitly state runtime Design Compiler / layout engine is not implemented; pageState is a declarative anchor only. No import, type reference, value declaration, or runtime read of UIDesignState exists in apps/ or packages/.
knownFindings.currentEffect: UIDesignState is advisory/specification; no runtime enforcement.
knownFindings.risk: Contract exists but is not machine-enforced; drift-check cannot validate it because page.state.ts files do not exist.
knownFindings.directEvidence: design-state.ts lines 11-12; coverage-counts.json UIDesignState counts all zero.

knownFindings.originalClaim: drift-check.mjs skips views when page.state.ts is absent, allowing empty-set success
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: drift-check.mjs line 263: if (!existsSync(statePath)) continue. With zero page.state.ts files and only 3 data-archetype views, the archetype drift loop never compares any pair and exits successfully.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Drift check reports success even though no page contract is validated.
knownFindings.risk: False confidence in page-contract coverage.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Existing scaffold generates route, view, and hook files but does not generate a complete page contract
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: ai-route-view-scaffold.mjs emits hook, view (with data-archetype), and route module. It never emits page.state.ts or imports UIDesignState.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Newly scaffolded pages lack the UIDesignState contract the design-state rule requires.
knownFindings.risk: Scaffold produces pages that technically violate 04-design-state-contract.mdc.
knownFindings.directEvidence: ai-route-view-scaffold.mjs files array lines 62-96; renderView lines 176-296.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Existing scaffold supports only table, form, and detail kinds
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: ai-route-view-scaffold.mjs allowedKinds = new Set(["table", "form", "detail"]).
knownFindings.currentEffect: Page archetypes like dashboard, settings, or data-management are not first-class scaffold kinds.
knownFindings.risk: New pages for unsupported intents require hand-written boilerplate.

knownFindings.originalClaim: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are ignored compatibility outputs rather than tracked cold-start entrypoints
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: Both files are listed in .gitignore lines 126-127. AGENTS.md is generated from .ai/protocol/AI.entry.md; CLAUDE.md is generated from AI.entry.md and contains only a pointer.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Fresh clone lacks tracked cold-start files until pnpm ai:sync runs.
knownFindings.risk: Cold-start depends on running generator or on AI client tolerating missing ignored files.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Claude currently receives a short pointer to AGENTS.md rather than a complete independent entry
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: CLAUDE.md contains only a link to AGENTS.md and a note that AGENTS.md is the shared entrypoint. adapter-manifest.json claude.discovery states the same.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Claude cold-start depends on AGENTS.md being generated and present.
knownFindings.risk: If AGENTS.md is stale or missing, Claude has no standalone protocol guidance.

knownFindings.originalClaim: pnpm ai:sync can replace adapter targets and remove repository .cursor
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: ai-sync.mjs calls runNodeScript on generators, syncFile copies AI.entry.md to AGENTS.md, ensureLocalRuntimeFile creates repair_list.md, and removePathIfExists removes .cursor legacy path.
knownFindings.currentEffect: ai:sync mutates ignored adapter files and removes legacy .cursor directory.
knownFindings.risk: Running ai:sync in P1 would violate read-only scope and alter ignored outputs.

knownFindings.originalClaim: AppContainer places a route-level CScrollbar around routed content
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: apps/web-demo/src/layouts/components/AppContainer.vue wraps AnimateRouterView in <CScrollbar class="col-fill">.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Route-level scrolling is owned by AppContainer/CScrollbar.
knownFindings.risk: None; consistent with scroll ownership rules.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Existing rules and examples contain conflicting scroll guidance
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: 04-design-state-contract.mdc archetype skeleton examples contain overflow-auto but the same section explicitly marks them illustrative ONLY and requires CScrollbar in production. No runtime layout file contradicts the CScrollbar mandate.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Documentation examples contain legacy overflow-auto wording but are guarded by explicit production guidance.
knownFindings.risk: Low; illustrative examples could confuse AI if not read in context.
knownFindings.contradictoryEvidence: 04-design-state-contract.mdc line 65 explicitly states overflow-auto examples are illustrative ONLY.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Existing UI preflight uses the vw/vh greater than percentage greater than px priority, which conflicts with the planned token-first model
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: 02-ui-preflight.mdc line 13 states priority is vw/vh > % > px. This is consistent with current rules but may conflict with a future token-first migration.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Current rule enforces viewport-relative units over tokens.
knownFindings.risk: Future project-ui profile that is token-first will conflict unless rules are updated.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Current Architectural Glass language mixes platform invariants with CCD product-profile decisions
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: ccd-product-language/SKILL.md, ccd-material-system/SKILL.md, and 00-unocss-guardrails.mdc blend reusable platform rules (token engine, closed shortcut registry, CScrollbar) with CCD-specific visual preferences (glass panels, liquid glass, Apple-like/Google-like translation).
knownFindings.currentEffect: Future migration must separate reusable platform invariants from CCD product profile.
knownFindings.risk: A future generic project-ui skill could inherit CCD-specific decisions or discard reusable ones.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Underlying design-token, UnoCSS, UI component, size, breakpoint, and theme foundations are reusable and should not be rebuilt
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: packages/design-tokens, packages/unocss-preset, packages/vue-ui, packages/vue-primevue-adapter, packages/vue-hooks, and packages/vue-app-platform provide theme, size, breakpoint, layout, motion, and component primitives with tests and clear governance references.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Foundations are reusable across CCD and future projects.
knownFindings.risk: Rebuilding would waste effort and risk regression.
knownFindings.directEvidence: platform-foundation-evidence.json; package READMEs and test files.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Current rule index records glob strings but does not prove that they match tracked files
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: rule-index.json stores globs from rule frontmatter but has no matchCount or validation field. glob-evidence.json independently shows 55 zero-match globs and 5 partial globs.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Rule index is a static catalog; coverage proof is external.
knownFindings.risk: Dead or partial globs persist without automated detection.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Current governance gate does not provide complete UI policy, page-contract coverage, routing precision, or scroll-ownership enforcement
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: governance:validate checks governance assets and protocol versions but does not validate rule glob coverage, page.state.ts presence, scroll-ownership compliance, or motion routing. ai-doctor checks adapter freshness and skill routing but does not enforce these UI policies.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Validation claims are narrower than the full UI governance surface.
knownFindings.risk: Gaps in enforcement may be mistaken for complete coverage.
knownFindings.directEvidence: governance:validate output (passed) in P1.1; ai-doctor.mjs source.

knownFindings.originalClaim: .codex/config.toml represents an execution-security owner decision and must remain outside automatic UI retirement
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: .codex/config.toml is the local Codex CLI execution-security and owner-decision surface. It controls provider, model, permission mode, and hook configuration for the local Codex runtime. AGENTS.core.md and the repository AI protocol treat .codex/ as a local runtime boundary that must not be modified by generic UI governance changes.
knownFindings.currentEffect: Automatic UI retirement process must exclude this file.
knownFindings.risk: If treated as UI governance, retirement could break local Codex execution.
knownFindings.evidence: File path .codex/config.toml follows Codex CLI convention; no repository script references it; AGENTS.core.md load order does not include it; it is outside the .ai/ governance tree and outside the P1 UI retirement scope.

knownFindings.originalClaim: Accepted P0 cleanup remains intact and unchanged
knownFindings.verifiedStatement: LOCAL_HEAD remains 9fad207b; P0 parent fdb8030a is in history; working tree clean; no P0 files modified.
knownFindings.currentEffect: P0 accepted state preserved.
knownFindings.risk: None.
knownFindings.directEvidence: git log -5; git status clean; git diff empty.

conflicts.description: adapter-manifest.json codex.skillMapping includes ccd-page-archetypes in the design skill chain, but skill-routing.json route ccd-ui-design omits it.
04-design-state-contract.mdc requires every page to declare a sibling page.state.ts UIDesignState contract, but ai-route-view-scaffold.mjs emits only a view, a hook, and a route module.

rule-index.json records old single-root globs (src/**) that generate-rule-index.mjs copied from rule frontmatter, but the generated manifest does not validate that those globs match current tracked files.

conflicts.conflictClass: old path versus monorepo path mismatch
conflicts.description: 47 unique old single-root patterns (src/**, index.html, vite.config.ts, src-tauri/**, build/**) produce zero or wrong-root matches because source code moved to apps/**/src/**.

conflicts.conflictClass: platform invariant versus product profile mixing
conflicts.description: Skills mix reusable platform invariants (token usage, spacing, motion safety) with CCD-specific product-profile decisions (Architectural Glass, CCD signature detail).

conflicts.conflictClass: motion default versus conditional motion conflict
conflicts.description: Generic UI route ccd-ui-design loads both GSAP and animate-lite motion skills by default; governance intends conditional loading (GSAP only for timeline work, animate-lite for route/class transitions).
UIDesignState contract exists and is required by rule, but no runtime consumer imports or reads it; page.state.ts files do not exist.
drift-check.mjs claims to validate archetype drift, but it silently continues when a view lacks a sibling page.state.ts, exiting 0 with zero comparisons.

conflicts.conflictClass: generated adapter versus cold-start discovery conflict
conflicts.description: AI client cold-start files AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are generated, ignored outputs. A fresh clone lacks them until ai:sync runs, but ai-doctor and codex-preflight require them.

GSAP skill forbids login/static-background motion and limits login to Level 1 motion by default, but LoginShell.vue and AuthShaderBackdrop.vue use GSAP timelines for login entrance and background bubble drift.
Both the design-state contract rule and the page-archetypes skill anchor page-level structural decisions, but neither fully owns the other and neither is machine-enforced.
Skills require reduced-motion handling and accessibility review, but no rule, lint rule, or test enforces prefers-reduced-motion or aria practices.
